activities_projects.title: Constituent Assembly Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the debates and discussions involved in drafting the Constitution of India
steps: Divide the class into groups representing members of the Constituent Assembly
Assign roles such as Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, Jawaharlal Nehru, and other leaders
Choose a topic like fundamental rights or secularism
Conduct a mock debate discussing different viewpoints
Summarize the final decision taken by the assembly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the democratic process and debates that shaped the Indian Constitution
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation, historical understanding, and quality of arguments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: States Reorganisation Policy Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the political and linguistic factors behind the States Reorganisation Act of 1956
steps: Study the background of linguistic demands in different regions
Mark the major states reorganized in 1956 on the map
Research reasons for reorganization
Write a detailed explanation about how linguistic identity influenced political decisions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically analyze how regional identity shaped India's administrative structure
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and accuracy of map work (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Partition Rehabilitation Research Project
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the government's response to refugee rehabilitation after Partition
steps: Research the migration caused by Partition
Identify government programs for refugee rehabilitation
Study examples of refugee settlements in cities like Delhi
Prepare a detailed report analyzing the success and challenges of these policies
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the long-term social and economic impact of Partition
activities_projects.evaluation: Research quality and analytical explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Five-Year Plans Evaluation
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the effectiveness of India's early Five-Year Plans
steps: Study the objectives of the First, Second, and Third Five-Year Plans
Identify their major achievements and limitations
Compare agricultural and industrial growth during these plans
Prepare a chart summarizing the outcomes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students critically evaluate economic planning in independent India
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison and clarity of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Green Revolution Impact Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the economic and environmental consequences of the Green Revolution
steps: Study how high-yield variety seeds and irrigation improved food production
Identify regions that benefited the most
Analyze issues such as soil degradation and inequality among farmers
Write an analytical report discussing both benefits and challenges
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking about agricultural modernization
activities_projects.evaluation: Balanced analysis and evidence-based reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Language Policy Debate
activities_projects.objective: Examine the role of language in national unity and regional identity
steps: Study the debates on official language in India
Divide students into groups supporting different viewpoints
Prepare arguments about Hindi, regional languages, and multilingual policy
Conduct a structured debate and summarize conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand complexities of language politics in India

activities_projects.title: Science and Technology Development Report
activities_projects.objective: Investigate India's growth in science and technology institutions after independence
steps: Research institutions such as ISRO, DRDO, and atomic energy programs
Identify major scientific achievements
Analyze how these developments contributed to national progress
Prepare a detailed written report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of scientific advancement in national development
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Women and Social Reform Study
activities_projects.objective: Examine the role of women in shaping post-independence Indian society
steps: Research prominent women leaders and reformers
Study their contributions in politics, education, or social movements
Analyze how their work influenced Indian society
Prepare a report discussing their impact
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the significance of gender equality and women's leadership
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and critical analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Urbanization and Development Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how urbanization shaped India's economy after independence
steps: Study reasons for urban growth after independence
Observe infrastructure and services in nearby towns or cities
Compare urban and rural development patterns
Write an analytical report explaining the causes and impacts of urbanization
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how economic growth influenced urban expansion

activities_projects.title: National Integration Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Study how India maintained unity despite cultural and regional diversity
steps: Research challenges to national unity such as regional movements or conflicts
Study government policies that promoted unity
Analyze examples where cooperation helped resolve issues
Prepare a case study report explaining lessons learned
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of unity and democratic institutions in maintaining national integration
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and clarity of explanation (10 marks)
